articles.title: Rupee falls 16 paise to open at 94.90 against US dollar

articles.title: Gold rate crashes over 1% on MCX as US dollar index hits over 1-year high; experts highlight key levels to watch
articles.summary: Gold rate crashed by over 1% on MCX as the US dollar index jumped to a 1-year high. MCX gold August futures were 1.21% down at ₹1,44,759 per 10 grams, while MCX silver July futures were 0.71% down at ₹2,24,227 per kg around 9:10 am.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: BSE to Marico - Jay Thakkar suggests 3 stocks to buy or sell for short-term in F&O segment
articles.summary: The Indian stock market may open flat amid mixed global cues and a significant selloff in tech stocks. The Nifty 50 closed below 24,000, marking a sideways to negative trend unless it regains this level. BSE, Marico, and Mankind Pharma show potential for movement.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Why the NSE IPO may not create wealth like BSE’s did
articles.summary: The NSE IPO arrives after a decade-long boom in Indian markets that enriched existing shareholders. While the exchange remains dominant and highly profitable, matching the stellar returns delivered by BSE since 2017 may prove far tougher.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: CSM Technologies IPO Day 1: Issue opens today. Check GMP, key dates, review. Apply or not?
articles.summary: CSM Technologies IPO is set from June 24-29, 2026, with an anchor investor raise of ₹20 crore. The price band is ₹107-113 per share, aimed at qualified institutional and retail investors. Listing expected on 2 July, with mixed brokerage views on the offering.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Buy or sell: Vaishali Parekh recommends three intraday stocks for today - 24 June 2026
articles.summary: Buy or sell stocks, 24 June 2026: Vaishali Parekh recommends three stocks to buy today — Mahindra Logistics, Reliance Industries, and Genus Power Infrastructures.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stock market today:Gift Nifty hints muted start;US tech stocks sell-off, Kospi rebounds to oil prices - 8 stocks to buy
articles.summary: Indian stock market indices Sensex and Nifty 50 are expected to open subdued due to mixed global signals and recent technology stock sell-offs. Investor sentiment remains cautious ahead of US-Iran negotiations, impacting the market outlook amid ongoing volatility in equities.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stocks to watch: Vedanta, IRFC, Yes Bank among shares in focus today; check list here
articles.summary: Stock market today: On Tuesday, Sensex tumbled 893.39 points, or 1.16%, to end the session at 76,200.68, while the NSE Nifty 50 declined 278.80 points, or 1.16%, to close at 23,824.10.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Nifty 50, Sensex prediction today: Check how Indian stock market is expected to trade on 24 June
articles.summary: Nifty 50, Sensex prediction today: The trends on Gift Nifty also indicate a muted start for the Indian benchmark index. The Gift Nifty was trading around 23,868 level, a premium of nearly 15 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Crude oil prices extend fall on smoother flows through Strait of Hormuz; Brent below $77 per barrel
articles.summary: US-Iran peace talks: Global benchmark Brent crude futures for August delivery declined 0.45% to $76.73 per barrel on Wednesday after falling 1.1% in the previous session.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: From Gift Nifty, Nasdaq selloff, US dollar to oil prices: 10 key things that changed for Indian stock market overnight
articles.summary: Gift Nifty was trading around 23,874 level, a premium of nearly 21 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close, indicating a muted start for the Indian stock market indices.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Asian stocks today: Kospi rebounds 3% after crash, Nikkei falls amid global tech stocks sell-off
articles.summary: Asian stocks today: The MSCI Asia Pacific Index rose 0.8% after tumbling 3.6% on Tuesday, marking its steepest decline since early March.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Stock recommendations for 24 June from MarketSmith India
articles.summary: MarketSmith India reveals its top stock recommendations for today, 24 June. Get expert insights into the best-performing stocks to guide your investment decisions.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Breakout stocks to buy or sell: Sumeet Bagadia recommends five shares to buy today - 24 June 2026
articles.summary: The Indian stock market ended Tuesday's session on a lower note. Sumeet Bagadia of Choice Broking recommends five breakout stocks to buy today.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Raja Venkatraman recommends three stocks for 24 June
articles.summary: Market expert Raja Venkatraman shares his top stock picks for 24 June. Here’s his technical outlook and trade strategy.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: PE-backed firms ride NSE, Jio tailwind to launch IPO roadshows
articles.summary: Investment bankers are preparing IPO roadshows for at least five to six PE-backed firms, including InCred, Dhoot Transmission and Integris Medtech, riding on improved liquidity sentiment after National Stock Exchange and Jio's DRHP filings.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Is IDBI Bank stock undervalued? It all hinges on the government’s potential stake sale.
articles.summary: While the bank is classified as private on paper, it still trades at heavily discounted public-sector multiples. But if the government pulls off its long-awaited divestment, a sharp re-rating could finally be unlocked.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: YES Bank board to consider fundraise via equity and debt securities
articles.summary: Yes Bank's board will meet on June 29, 2026, to consider capital raising through equity and debt instruments. The proposals aim to provide flexibility for future fundraising aligned with regulatory requirements, while seeking shareholder approvals at the upcoming AGM.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Kalshi adds India to restricted list after prediction market ban
articles.summary: Kalshi Inc. has restricted users in India from trading event contracts after the country's tech ministry deemed prediction markets illegal. The updated member agreement prohibits users from India, following a move to block access to the platform since May.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Gold, silver rates today: Comex gold and silver extend losses for third straight day as Fed rate-hike bets rise
articles.summary: Comex gold and silver prices continued to decrease as market expectations of a Fed rate hike strengthened the US dollar. Gold futures fell to $4,108, and silver dropped to $61.53, with silver experiencing an 18% loss in June alone and declining nearly 50% from its January peak.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Sebi proposes ad rules for MF, stock brokers: Celebrities may endorse brands, not products | What's allowed, what's not
articles.summary: Sebi proposed new advertising rules allowing financial entities to use celebrities for brand promotion, not specific products. The Common Advertisement Code aims for consistent regulations, eliminating prior approval and enabling post-issuance monitoring.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Waterways Leisure Tourism IPO: Issue receives tepid response on Day 01, subscribed 0.20x; GMP signals flat listing
articles.summary: Waterways Leisure Tourism's IPO opened on June 23 but saw low interest, being subscribed only 0.20 times. A total of 8.14 lakh shares were bid for against 39.82 lakh available. The company aims to raise ₹585 crore, with a market valuation of ₹5,849.48 crore post-issue.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: SpaceX falls below IPO price before rebounding 2%: Is it a second-chance buying opportunity?
articles.summary: SpaceX shares rebounded 2% after falling below $150, impacting its market valuation under $2 trillion. After a $75 billion IPO, investor sentiment weakened, with SpaceX losing over $600 billion in value post-IPO. Despite the volatility, shares remain 10% above the $135 IPO price.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Micron Technology shares plunge 13% as global chip selloff deepens amid AI valuation concerns
articles.summary: Micron Technology shares dropped 13% amid concerns over chip stock valuations and slowing AI memory chip production by SK Hynix, leading to a significant decline in semiconductor stocks.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Market could be on cusp of trend reversal; business cycle funds suitable for capturing sector opportunities, say experts
articles.summary: As the Indian stock market hints at a trend reversal, experts advocate for business cycle funds that promise diversified investments across shifting sectors. With strong recent performances, these funds allow investors to seize new opportunities within an evolving economy.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Advit Jewels IPO: Issue received solid response on Day 01, booked 11.3 times; GMP signals bumper listing
articles.summary: Advit Jewels' IPO, valued at ₹165.16 crore, opened on June 23 and received bids for 9.45 crore shares, marking an overall subscription of 11.28 times. The retail segment was most popular, attracting 27 times the bids. The IPO runs until June 25.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: US stock market today: Nasdaq, S&P 500 futures tumble up to 3% as tech selloff deepens; Micron slides 7%
articles.summary: US stock futures indicated a steep decline on June 23, amid a tech sell-off led by SK Hynix's production cuts. The Nasdaq fell over 1.3%, affecting global markets. Concerns over AI spending and potential interest rate hikes further weighed on investor sentiment.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Top Gainers & Losers on June 23: Vedanta, Hindustan Zinc, Infosys, TCS, Bank of Baroda among top losers
articles.summary: The Indian stock market fell sharply on June 23, influenced by global market weakness and a retreat in technology stocks. The Nifty 50 declined 1.16%, marking its biggest drop in nearly four weeks, with all sectoral indices closing lower amid profit-booking and a stronger US dollar.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Sensex crashes almost 900 points, investors lose ₹6 lakh crore in a day: What drove the stock market down?
articles.summary: The Sensex lost 893 points, or 1.16%, to end at 76,200.68, while the Nifty 50 shed 279 points, or 1.16%, to settle at 23,824.10.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Hindustan Zinc shares drop 5% amid slump in global silver prices
articles.summary: Hindustan Zinc shares fell 5% amid declining global silver prices, reaching an intraday low of ₹544. Concerns over inflation and potential rate hikes persist as investors assess the US economic outlook and upcoming PCE data.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: FIIs remain net sellers in Indian stock market for 3 months in a row. Can the trend change?
articles.summary: On 19 June, FIIs purchased equities worth ₹31,442.87 crore and sold shares worth ₹26,583.80 crore, leading to net inflows of ₹4,859.07 crore.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Is AI euphoria an overlooked risk for markets beyond geopolitical shocks?
articles.summary: As AI stocks surge, experts warn of the risks associated with excessive optimism. Transitioning to a value-driven investment strategy focused on fundamentals rather than narratives is crucial. Discover the balance needed to navigate the exhilarating yet perilous landscape of AI investments.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Is Kirloskar Oil Engines stock running ahead of fundamentals after 25% jump this week?
articles.summary: Kirloskar Oil Engines shares hit a 52-week high after winning a 192MW data-centre genset order from HyperNext, breaking into a segment dominated by Cummins. Strong FY26 numbers, capacity expansion and a narrowing valuation gap versus Cummins are fuelling the re-rating.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Jio Platforms IPO: RIL to not levy royalty for 'Jio' brand
articles.summary: RIL has decided to depart from a common practice among Indian conglomerates, in which promoter entities charge a fee calculated based on the operating company's financials.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: IDFC First Bank share price falls over 2% amid weak sentiment in Indian stock market
articles.summary: IDFC First Bank share price has risen 14% in one month and has rallied over 30% in three months. The banking stock has fallen 7% in six months, while it has gained 11% in one year. IDFC First Bank shares have surged 36% in five years.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Businesses embrace GST, but seek more reforms: Deloitte survey
articles.summary: The findings come at a time when GST collections continue to be robust. In May, the Centre and states together collected ₹1.94 trillion in gross GST revenue, before adjusting for refunds, up 3.2% from ₹1.88 trillion mopped up a year ago.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Why India’s consumers may stay cautious despite easing oil prices
articles.summary: Falling crude oil prices offer hope, but delayed rains, food inflation and higher living costs continue to weaken consumer confidence.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Millets, check dams and emergency plans: How India is preparing for dry days
articles.summary: Low monsoon rainfall is significant, as the season accounts for about 70% of India's annual rainfall. The rural economy, which relies heavily on agriculture, remains an important driver of economic growth.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: RBI allows banks to give loans for FCNR deposits
articles.summary: On 5 June, RBI said it will absorb the foreign exchange hedging cost on fresh  CNR (B), deposits, creating an unusual opportunity for overseas Indians
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Moving decisively towards finalizing trade deal with India, says US Ambassador Gor
articles.summary: India and the US are moving closer to finalizing a bilateral trade agreement, with commerce minister Piyush Goyal and USTR Jamieson Greer holding talks in New Delhi. Negotiations focus on tariffs, market access and digital trade ahead of a key US tariff deadline in July.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India's private sector output moderates further in June
articles.summary: June saw slower growth in new orders, international sales, employment and business activity.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Will Singapore remain India's export star even after Iran war ends?
articles.summary: West Asian geopolitical tensions have driven a surge in India’s exports to Singapore, utilizing its robust transshipment infrastructure to diversify from traditional hubs. Will this continue?
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt plans subsidized tomato sales to cool price surge
articles.summary: In several local retail markets, tomatoes are currently being sold at ₹60-70 per kg. Retail tomato prices have jumped in India due to delays in arrivals and weather-related disruptions in producing regions.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: US trade chief to visit India for interim deal discussions
articles.summary: The visit comes amid efforts by India and the US to deepen economic ties and address market access issues through a comprehensive trade pact.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: RBI governor asks banks to treat MSMEs as long-term partners
articles.summary: The RBI governor asked financial institutions to make greater use of India's digital public infrastructure
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: RBI flags risks if West Asia peace deal fails, says India remains strong
articles.summary: RBI's assessment comes amid easing tensions between the US and Iran, which has helped moderate concerns around energy supply disruptions and global trade routes
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: India's core sector growth hits seven-month low as Iran war bites
articles.summary: Given the tepid core sector output in May 2026, India's IIP growth is likely to weaken to 2-3% in the month from 4.9% in April 2026, according to an analyst.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Gen Z is out-earning millennials, shows data; but has inflation already closed the gap?
articles.summary: New research shows Gen Z workers are out-earning millennials at the same age — but with soaring rents in the UK, US, and India, higher wages may not mean better lives. Here's what the data really shows.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: From village adulteration to organized food crime: The evolution of India’s fake milk industry
articles.summary: Evidence is emerging that in some northern states and Gujarat people are being sold fake milk that looks and tastes like the real thing. This deep dive looks at the growing sophistry in adulterating milk and how consumers can protect themselves.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The benefits and risks of RBI’s policy on foreign currency NRI deposits
articles.summary: To shore up the rupee, the government is looking at NRIs to make foreign currency deposits in Indian banks. This has implications for India’s external debt management.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India, Iran look to soon resume talks to get Chabahar Port plan back on track
articles.summary: India and Iran may resume discussions on restoring normal operations at the strategic Chabahar Port and reviving plans to expand the facility, as geopolitical conditions in the region show signs of stabilising, according to two people aware of the matter.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Young Punjab kids ‘rich for 4 yrs after selling their lands… then broke’: World Bank's Ajay Banga on urban agri economy
articles.summary: World Bank President Ajay Banga pointed out challenges in urban and agricultural economies, particularly in Punjab, where youth selling farmland quickly spend their earnings, leading to financial instability.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Views, virality and CJP protest: How India's creator economy found a new stage at Jantar Mantar
articles.summary: A May 2025 report, ‘From Content to Commerce: Mapping India’s Creator Economy’ by BCG, said that with over 2–2.5 million monetised content creators influencing more than $350–400 billion in consumer spending, the ecosystem is projected to drive $1 trillion+ in creator-influenced consumption by 2030.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The week in charts: State debt concerns, WPI inflation, NSE’s IPO debut
articles.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Development finance should be used strategically to improve governance, service delivery in Northeast: FM Sitharaman
articles.summary: Finance minister Nirmala Sitharaman said that externally aided project funding to India's Northeast has risen nearly eightfold to ₹76,000 crore since 2014, and urged states to use such finance to strengthen governance and service delivery.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: AI boom shields Asia from West Asia shock, but risks mount for India and region: Moody’s Analytics
articles.summary: Moody's Analytics has said that AI-led exports and investment have helped Asia-Pacific weather the fallout from the West Asia conflict, but higher oil prices, inflation, trade tensions and weather-related disruptions could slow growth in the years ahead.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Watchful and wary: Inside MPC's unanimous decision to hold rates
articles.summary: At its 3-5 June meeting, the Monetary Policy Committee kept the key repo rate unchanged at 5.25%, while acknowledging that there were considerable risks to its assessments of inflation and growth.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Oil slides below $80 as US-Iran peace deal eases supply fears
articles.summary: Brent slipped below $80 a barrel as the US and Iran signed a peace framework, easing supply concerns and paving the way for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Fuel curbs rollback likely as Iran deal raises hope
articles.summary: The Centre is weighing a rollback of emergency curbs on fuel sales imposed during the West Asia conflict after the US and Iran sued for peace, a move that could ease pressure on farmers, lower inflation and improve prospects for the world's third-largest oil importer.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India secures steel market access under UK FTA; exporters to avail benefits from 15 July
articles.summary: India has secured quota-based protection for its steel exports under the India-UK Ceta, ensuring market access is preserved even as Britain's safeguard measures take effect from 1 July. The broader trade pact will come into force on 15 July.
articles.label: BULLISH
